channel.title: Comments for page "The Knight of Flowers"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Knight of Flowers"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved this. Very easy +1 for me.

edit: typo addressed

item.title: Site Crit - Swamp Critter
item.content:encoded:
Hi, this is Cyvstvi13 and I'll be performing a criticism on this piece as part of the Swamp Critters Squad.

+ Crit
- Crit

Overall

I absolutely loved this article to bits. The prose is solid as it gets and the interaction between the various Black Queens was both humourous and characterising. You get a real sense of hierarchy between Erasmus, Ariadne, and Gaia, still pondering if their names mean anything important.

SPaG

Only one possible error and it might even be intentional :

"Able ben Adam" —> "Able Ben Adam".

Consistency/Flow

The article had a great consistency when it came to both tone and flow. The article really gels from one paragraph to the next and the overall progression works wonderfully.

The twist stroke embedded information about Gaia that is slowly worked up is really clever and I enjoyed it immensely, makes a second read that much more fulfilling than the first one.

Closing Remarks

I don't have anything negative to express at all in this piece, I absolutely loved it to bits and gave it a +1. We always need more Black Queen stuff.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to Cyvstvi13 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, at first I was a bit on the no vote side. I dont know BQ canon at all, so some of this doesn't make sense to me.

However, I think the writing is solid, and I enjoyed the interplay between the "queens" enough to make this upvote worthy.

+1

item.title: Up the airy mountain, down the rushy glen
item.content:encoded:
My GOI format, for the last day of Jamcon 2020: More than meets the eye.

Spoilers
Spoilers

Gaia is Gaia. But they weren't always Gaia.

Poem is The Loreley, by Heinrich Heine. Image is from here.
